dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena, by your leave... we have a mutual acquaintance I would ask about, because... well... do you recall the weaver Arachne?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I seldom forget anything, Cousin. Especially not insolent mortals who see themselves as equals to the gods. Though she is not exactly mortal anymore. What of her?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... she lives in Erebus now, near where I reside, and... she's so wretched about her appearance, which I understand is from a curse you wrought... doubtless entirely deserved, but... have mercy on her, Lady Athena. Please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So many mortals grovel to me all the time. But goddesses? And on behalf of mortals far too prideful to grovel for themselves? Cousin, do not waste my time with this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The mortal woman called Arachne. Have you any idea what she did? She fancied herself the greatest weaver in the world; superior even to me, if not the Fates themselves. Is it not our obligation, then, to remind mortals of their place?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... can't say I rightly know the answer to that, Lady Athena. Nor would I ever question your decision about her! I only ask if the term of her punishment could be less than eternal. She's humbled, and she's learned from her mistakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Has she, now? In my experience mortals can be very slow to learn. And she was not punished as a lesson to her, but as a lesson to everyone else. I do not go back on my decisions, Melinoë. For I don't make them lightly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your thoughts about Arachne are causing you distraction, which in turn is causing me distraction now. I would propose a means by which we could put this matter with her to rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? Please let me know what you have in mind, Lady Athena. Arachne is a friend, and if there's some way I could help her, I would. I already know the source of the curse...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: And so you need for me to let it lapse. And while you cannot compel me, perhaps Arachne can, if she is now as different as you say. You know my Gorgon Amulet? Ask if she would speak with me through it. If so, I may make an appearance.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena, I... I don't know what to say, except... I deeply regret the incident between you and Arachne. I was certain she sought your forgiveness, not an altercation.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I am less surprised by the outcome, but then I knew Arachne from before. Perhaps she did mean to apologize, until the moment she saw me again and all her scorn came rushing back. Nonetheless, what's done is done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I understand. Though, if I may ask... what did she do to have incurred your judgment? I know a weaving contest was involved, but only from her side of the tale.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Losing to some mortal was a humiliation I did not anticipate. Yet it was Arachne's arrogance that sparked my wrath, rather than any envy I had for her considerable talent. She had the nerve to call my work pedestrian.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She insulted you... that was short-sighted, certainly, and as I understand she was quite young. Had she but known...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: This last time, she did know. Yet she insulted me again. We do not expect much from mortals; merely penitence. For those such as Arachne, the best we can do is make examples of them. Now I shall spare her not another word.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My half-brother Ares must be having the time of his life, wherever he resides. He relishes a good protracted war... why would he work with us to put an end to it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you suspect betrayal? If Ares isn't here, and the war rages... perhaps he's stoking the conflict on the other side.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The thought has certainly occurred to me, except... our enemy is no more interested in a war that lasts forever than we are. Whereas Ares wants only for the war to perpetuate, not for a victor to emerge.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know who else wants that is Eris. She's mentioned Ares before. You don't suppose they're working together?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: From what I know of Strife, she's not prone to alliances. Yet, I cannot rule out the possibility. She's been impeding you along your route... perhaps she'll let slip some detail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So Ares has decided to participate in our family activities after all. Ever willing to compromise! For if one everlasting war cannot be had, he'd settle for an outcome that ensures more wars are still to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know your views of war are not the same as his. But I will not decline his power on the basis of beliefs. Which means I'm willing to compromise, too... if doing so brings victory.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Be careful with that line of thought, Cousin. Compromising to achieve consensus is one thing; but compromising on beliefs or principles... on ideals? Such reasoning is how wars often start, not merely end.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm yet to be convinced that these Automatons we use in our defense are as dependable as can be. I've told Hephaestus I won't tolerate their quirks; nor should you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They are remarkable, but seem unable to fully distinguish your allies from your enemies. Perhaps Hephaestus can make improvements to their design.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, that's on a long list of requests we have for him. We'll have to decommission and repurpose any existing models for that effort anyhow... so if you destroy some here and there, it's for the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Cousin, is it true that you reached the heart of your father's Underworld and vanquished the Titan Chronos there? His forces unexpectedly fell back, and I dared not trust my intuition as to why.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I did prevail against him recently, yes. One night we shall dispose of him entirely, but for now, small victories shall have to suffice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Imperative victories are not small victories. Yet I fully understand the impulse never to grow complacent, and always to fight on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: There's some strange spell at work on you, for you seem different than before. Much... more substantial. Whether a blessing or a curse, I can't immediately say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a blessing, or at least I think it is. The craft of Madame Circe the Enchantress, whom I encountered on my voyage here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, her I do recall. She once created quite the predicament for a mortal seafarer whom I intended to protect, no one I think you'd know. I'd watch myself with her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: There's some strange spell at work on you, for you seem different than before. Have you... shrunk down, Cousin? Whether a blessing or a curse, I can't immediately say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a blessing, or at least I think it is. The craft of Madame Circe the Enchantress, whom I encountered on my voyage here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, her I do recall. She once created quite the predicament for a mortal seafarer whom I intended to protect, no one I think you'd know. I'd watch myself with her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You appear different than before. Another one of the enchantress Circe's unconventional blessings, then. Are you certain that she's aiding you, not... ridiculing you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Madame Circe would never use her transformation magick for ridicule. This is a powerful incantation... merely with certain side effects.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Never use her magick for ridicule? That doesn't sound much like the Circe I recall. Perhaps she's learned to behave herself, though I am not convinced...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You appear different than before. Another one of the enchantress Circe's unconventional blessings, then. Are you certain that she's aiding you, not... ridiculing you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Madame Circe would never use her transformation magick for ridicule. This is a powerful incantation... merely with certain side effects.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Never use her magick for ridicule? That doesn't sound much like the Circe I recall. Perhaps she's learned to behave herself, though I am not convinced...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My understanding was that your brother suffered from a certain ailment that inhibited his vitality if ever he attempted to venture out of the Underworld. Yet you apparently have no such limitation?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I merely found the means to slow the effect, but I can feel it encroaching even now. If our foes aren't able to drive me to retreat, my ailment eventually shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I say? You hide your troubles well, Melinoë. So yours is a race against time in more ways than one. I won't delay you any further then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I must say, Cousin... I've not seen such unity from our family in some time. Almost everybody stands together now against our foes. Though Dionysus, probably he's lying somewhere in a stupor.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If Lord Dionysus hasn't taken up arms against the forces of Chronos, surely he has his reasons...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, he does. Why should he callus his hands, when he could laze about feasting? He's fortunate I've more important matters to consider than locating him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Well at least it isn't snowing here. I thought such devastation would be concentrated on Olympus, but it has spread all the way to Ephyra.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Titan's legions may have ransacked this city for symbolic reasons... raising their banners while destroying any monuments to the Underworld King and Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Ransacking requires no premeditation or strategic purpose, and the result of it is clear. But what I fail to see is how this helps persuade mortals to side with Titans over us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: These must be the dark woods of Erebus, somewhere near your base of operations. I can see why they left such an impression on Artemis.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Welcome, Lady Athena! Perhaps Artemis feels somewhat at home in woods of any sort, even ones haunted by Lost Souls denied their eternal rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It's certainly a change from Mount Olympus. But I'm not here to survey the terrain, so quickly choose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The Three Fates themselves are gone, Hermes has said. Our situation grows that much more desperate, then. And yet... I never could depend on outside influence in my success; I trust you take no offense.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I understand what you mean. Chronos feared some sort of Fated prophecy would do him in again, like when our fathers overthrew his rule. But if the Fates no longer are setting the course of what transpires... then we shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: We shall. For the goal of any strategy is to leave as little as possible to chance. We count only on those who prove themselves dependable, and above all, ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh... these Fields of Mourning have some similarity to sprawling plains upon the surface, but the air hangs very heavy here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for locating me even in such a place, Lady Athena. I wouldn't advise staying for long even if you could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: And I wouldn't advise getting run through by my spear, yet at times it must be done. May your journey through this place take not a moment longer than it must.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So Artemis discovered you within the mists of Erebus, under the tutelage of Hecate? Quite the tale. Would you care to elaborate?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All I can say is Headmistress Hecate was under strict orders never to reveal me to anyone outside her inner circle. Not until our opportunity to strike at Chronos presented itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Very well. I was never under the delusion that our side of the family and yours kept no secrets from each other. Likely it's for the best. But, my position does require a certain thoroughness and curiosity, you understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My Stepmother Queen Hera's favor isn't easily earned. You should know it's she who first desired your assistance here. You have more than her Boon, but her faith; fragile as it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I am grateful for it, but... do you happen to know why? I don't know what Her Majesty the Queen could have seen in me, especially since we'd never even met.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps it's that you represent a different course of action than the one we'd previously decided on, which came directly from my father your Lord Uncle Zeus. None of us like to be wrong about anything, but... we like victory more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You have perhaps encountered mighty Heracles along your route. He has surpassing strength and capability, but do report to me any suspicious actions on his part.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have encountered him, and seen him lay to waste droves of our foes, though he's not been keen on conversation. Which sorts of suspicious actions do you mean?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Consorting with our foes, defying our will, anything of that sort. I have no real reason not to trust him, yet... I've come to trust you more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The mighty Heracles has remained silently obedient in all of this, expressing very little, which in turn tells me a lot. What's your assessment of him presently?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I... well, he's exceptionally strong, true to his reputation, but besides that he... does not seem on the best of terms with the King and Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm. He must feel that he has more to lose by violating their will than bending to it, but... a warrior such as that... we would be wiser to ensure he's treated well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: All of this warfare leaves us time for little else. I used to weave, you know. Great tapestries and more! May I have opportunity to take it up again once all this is behind us. And you, what else has occupied your time?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I, well... I like to spar! Improving my acuity and strength. And practicing my craft. Matters of magick, the living, and the dead. How they're connected.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm familiar with witchcraft, yes. Though, that all sounds like preparation for the matter you're now seeking to resolve! But I do realize it's not the time for flights of fancy, or even the discussion thereof.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear the Amulet I gave to you. May it protect you and strike fear into the hearts of your enemies, whenever I am not around to do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Lady Athena. The creature engraved on this... it's a Gorgon, isn't it? A guardian of some sort?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, not exactly, no. More of a cautionary reminder about what happens when we gods are openly defied. Don't lose your head out there, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You take me to the strangest places with that Gorgon Amulet, Cousin. I'm pleased you've gained such use from it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain you're aware that your blessings are extraordinarily valuable, Lady Athena. As long as my repeated summons haven't been too inconvenient.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: When it is inconvenient, I do not show up. Then let us keep charting this strange path together, not alone!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You got me thinking about mortalkind of late. I, too, have taken a personal interest in mortals and their plights. Many times. Especially in those who rise above their station. And I wish to honor their worship of the other gods and me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yet you said before that it's our obligation to remind them of their place... that they are ultimately powerless against us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, for they outnumber us greatly, and many grow resourceful and independent. Some do not think they need us, but they do. Both in life and in death, as your father would attest. Best not become too attached to any of them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been very well prepared... outfitted with excellent equipment, knowledgeable of the twists and turns through our mountain, not to mention even getting there. Whoever plotted your course is good at it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Lady Athena. We had to know what we were getting ourselves into given the precarity of the situation, so we planned as best we could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. Though I find such strategic insight is less likely to come from a collective we than from a single, sound mind. Nobody's business but your own, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The depths of Oceanus... I have often soared or sailed over these waters, but not often seen them from within. So this is how you circumvent Elysium.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: An indirect route to the lowest reaches, yes. Perhaps you'll frighten off some of the fishlike horrors.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Such base creatures have little capacity for fear, but we shall teach them of it what we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It almost looks as though you're having to contend with the invaders on your own, Lady Athena. There's a lot of mountain to cover, with too few allies...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, we have you, Cousin. As for our other allies... we as a family are loath to beg assistance, or for anything. To all our followers and compatriots, our message has been clear: We have this matter well under control.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is that the truth of it, or merely posturing, if you'll pardon my bluntness? You are clearly being overrun.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: On the contrary, our Automatons have quite effectively held many of our enemies at bay. And you and I can make a significant difference as well, if we don't dally.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My father's Palace is now closed for retrofitting, probably much like what's underway in your father's realm... leaving you with nowhere to rest after a hard night's work.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's all right, Lady Athena. I mustn't stay here longer than I have to, and recover far more swiftly where I'm from.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You fight as though you're unencumbered, but I know the surface weighs on you. It is impressive how you've learned to suppress this Fate-wrought weakness in your blood.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: After we settled certain troubles with your brother's situation at home, your mother Persephone, she would regularly return to Olympus for part of each year. I should have trusted my instincts when one day she failed to show...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Presumably when Chronos struck. Wouldn't her sudden disappearance have been cause for considerable alarm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd think. However, we knew your mother was with child, and our conclusion was that your new family wanted privacy. So we waited patiently, not realizing it as Chronos gained his foothold. A grave miscalculation on my part...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your father and mine first fought and vanquished Chronos long before either of us was born. You couldn't have expected the Titan Lord to suddenly return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Yet if I'm to be the strategist upon Olympus then I have to consider each possibility, however unlikely, that can threaten our peace. At any rate, a lesson harshly learned...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Beware ahead. The Titan Prometheus is able to elude me, knowing that I'm focused on his vanguard forces. But he'll have no choice but to confront you if you press on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's the Titan of Foresight. He knows exactly what we're planning, doesn't he? And can retaliate accordingly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: He knows what is to come to some extent, yes. But he can only see outcomes, not read minds. And just because he knows you're coming doesn't mean he can stop you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you confronted Prometheus before? You mentioned he's been able to elude you. Perhaps he knows he'd stand no chance against you if you fought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew Prometheus prior to all this. I suppose our tendencies toward matters of the mind connected us. You know that he stole the gift of fire for mortalkind. Well... he also gave them reason, from me. Some of them, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I shudder to think what mortals must have been like before they had any reason. Although perhaps Prometheus ought to have kept some reason for himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps he thinks he did, or has as much as he requires. Unfortunately, reason tends to be in lesser abundance than most would dare to admit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Even if you're thwarted by the Titan Prometheus, it is a benefit to our war effort. For if he's having to confront you, then he's unable to cause his mayhem elsewhere.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That is of little consolation to me, Lady Athena. This is not his first crime against Olympus and our family; thus his punishment is to be even more severe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not concern yourself with that. His prior punishment inspired this still-greater transgression, rather than remorse for his past deeds. For the moment, our strategy merely requires that he not stand in our way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's always good to see you, Lady Athena. You truly are relentless in this continued effort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Hardly an effort at this point, but good to see you likewise, Cousin. These meetings provide a welcome change from having to dispose of our enemies with extreme prejudice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The Moon herself assists you, that I know. We often underestimate her here, believing all she does is faintly light the sky. But I know there is more to her than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Selene may be a Titaness but she's an ally to us through and through. She upholds Night's will, as do I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx's will is none of my concern, Cousin. But apparently it's yours, and Nyx has likewise been an ally to Olympus in the past. If you trust Selene's involvement, that is enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: This night is darker than so many others of late, and our foes appear emboldened. Some new trick of Chronos, or... perhaps merely my imagination from fighting for so long.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It isn't your imagination but the will of Night herself, Lady Athena. And though the effect may seem to our detriment for now, we shall grow stronger for it, I assure you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Such assurances mean little to me typically, Cousin. But you know more about the darkness than I do. If this truly is the will of Night, and not the work of our enemy, then good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Such horrors have befallen our proud summit. This is intolerable... I ask myself how I might have prevented it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I would have no good answer, Lady Athena. Olympus is facing a coordinated attack on multiple fronts, from the air, earth, and sea. Your Automatons can only do so much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: But we can always do more even if those mindless machines cannot. We could have done more! For now, though... we had best focus on the resolution, not the cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So then it's true, what Chronos said... these possibilities that Typhon could have remained. Or perhaps the Gorgon Amulet is letting me share your hallucinations now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It all seems real enough to me... especially having retraced my path up to this point. But we can stop this here just as we did before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Then that is what we shall do. There is no reason to change strategies as long as they keep yielding good results.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you felt the growing tremors during your approach, Cousin? I don't care for them at all. Lord Uncle Poseidon at first acted as though they were his doing, but not even he seems to know the source...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In the Underworld, we experience tremors all the time, for the Earth is all around us, living and breathing. But I'm surprised such shaking could be felt all the way up here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps because it's more than the Earth living and breathing. Our enemy is behind this, I am certain of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I must apologize about your confrontation with the particularly large and dangerous Automaton called Talos. It detects traces of the Underworld on you, and, well...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a magnificent creation, and Lord Hephaestus should be proud... though, the methods by which his Automatons detect threats could probably use a comprehensive rework.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be certain to relay the suggestion. Unfortunately Lord Hephaestus prioritized a self-repairing system in Talos over threat-detection, so it may continue to be an issue for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: All this gold... what has become of Tartarus? The Titan Chronos rendered it almost entirely unrecognizable...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps when next I use the Gorgon Amulet to summon you here, you could ask him yourself, Lady Athena.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, would that I could and the Amulet were even stronger. But all strategies have limitations. My blessing rather than my presence shall have to do as you approach your goal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: This sailing-vessel's rotting deck... we are amid the Titan's fleet in Lord Uncle Poseidon's Rift. You use such means to reach our mountain every night?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... some nights I go to the Underworld instead, but otherwise, erm, yes. I've not had much success learning to fly or shift between the realms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I laud your ingenuity... you, or whoever plotted such a course, that turns the enemy's advance to an advantage.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You are a welcome sight, Melinoë, for it has been some nights since last we met upon the battlefield like this. How fares the war beneath the Earth?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It rages on. Night after night the Titan's legions regroup, while he asserts that he cannot be stopped. But I am working on disproving that. And what news here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The mountain stands, as you can see, and that is what matters. Perhaps your presence and a corresponding shift in strategy shall lead us to the breakthrough that we need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It has been longer than I'd like, Cousin! Our enemies abound, and lately I've dealt with more than my share.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Apologies, Lady Athena. Matters drew my attention from Olympus, though I was confident you and the others would hold fast while I was away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: And hold fast we did. But when you're here, I find we can go beyond merely defending, and properly fight back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You've seen what plagues us at the summit, haven't you? The source of all the tremors... almost as though the mountain itself quakes with fear. We need a plan...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The gods have stood against Typhon before. Wasn't it a well-placed thunderbolt from Zeus that did him in?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Tales of valor and deeds of valor are not always one and the same. Then let us lure this Typhon toward the summit, where our strength combined with yours is the best opportunity we have... and please take care, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I have been called a savior many times, and take some pleasure in overturning near-hopeless situations. But recently, it was my own hope that faltered... until you restored it. The Father of All Monsters is truly gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Olympus belongs to the gods... Typhon's destruction was a necessity. I may have been the one to deliver him to the void, but the doing of it took our combined strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You chthonic gods and your modesty. Be proud, Cousin! You have achieved a glorious result, for which we all are thankful. I knew I had to trust you. But I also felt I should.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank the Fates that you eluded Typhon's fury. And you created enough of a diversion that we were able to fortify our defenses and keep that monstrosity at bay, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But he'll be back. Have you discovered any weaknesses of note? I'd rather do more than distract him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: A being of that size will not have many weaknesses... although no creature likes to be jabbed in the eyes; and Typhon has a great many of them, for better or worse. And if we muster all our powers... perhaps we can drive him back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Cousin, after much consideration, I admit the best plan that I have for stopping Typhon at this point... is to rely on you. The next best alternative available would be most dire.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Uncle Zeus alluded to some force that could potentially defeat that monster, but bring great harm to Olympus in the process... is that the alternative you mean?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It is. My energies, as you can see, are focused on repelling our enemy's forces here. But it seems I shall also have to dissuade my father from taking a catastrophic course, if but to buy you time. I'm counting on you... we all are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: We were able to repel that monstrous Typhon thanks to your aid! So it can be done! Only the contemptible thing soon recovered and commenced his climb once more...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There has to be a way to vanquish Typhon utterly, not just slow him down. But if slowing him's the best result we can achieve for now... so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The so-called Father of All Monsters... I heard the tales many times of course, but seeing him... I felt my courage almost falter; which was more frightening even than the thing itself...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: That Typhon seems only to grow angrier each time we withstand his rage. A clever ploy for Chronos to set such a thing on us... keeping us occupied, while he plots his next move.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Whether clever or reckless, I'm not yet convinced. Chronos is counting on Typhon turning all his violence against Olympus, but after that...? Not even he can control such an abomination.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps Chronos means to seal the monster away once we are no longer a threat. At any rate, we don't intend to let any such outcome transpire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: First you vanquished Chronos in your father's realm, then in turn you toppled Typhon from our mountain's heights. I must admit, I underestimated you when first we met.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've trained my entire life for this eventuality, Lady Athena. It's what should be expected of me, and besides... our war isn't over yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You trained with Hecate to vanquish Chronos, that I know. But Typhon was never part of your practice routine. Yet I, too, dislike compliments especially when, to your point, our war is not yet won.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear the Arms of Night. I suspected it when we first met but now my doubt is gone. Should the Underworld be imperiled, those weapons are to be the last resort, fine. But they seem well outside their jurisdiction here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The peril that the Underworld faces evidently spread even to this mountain. For my part, the Nocturnal Arms have proven equally dependable above the surface and below.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Make certain that you keep their power well in check, as well as their hunger. I much prefer my weapons not to have intentions of their own, but can't deny their potency.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: How strange it is to meet you face-to-face. Your brother Zagreus I spoke to many times, though by Boon all but once. You must know he had his struggles at home. Now it seems your side of the family is repaying us for back then.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If there's a debt to be repaid then I am unaware, Lady Athena. I'm here because our family is in peril, my side and yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Of course, and it wasn't my intent to suggest otherwise. I'm merely reflecting on the change of circumstance. Well then, one Boon deserves another.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You didn't remind me of Zagreus at first but I do see some of it now. I've not had occasion to resume Boon messages to him, or for that matter you. I trust he's well?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He seems to be in good spirits, yes. Even though we met just recently, I feel as though I've known him for a while. How did the two of you first come into contact anyhow?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess Nyx is how. A long tale for another time but I thought she was his mother at first. Each of us was raised without a mother, come to think! Regards to all your kin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You should know, my father your Lord Uncle Zeus is somewhat skeptical of your ability to turn the outcome of the war here in our favor. He believes we should be able to fend for ourselves; and I don't disagree.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena, are we supposed to stand united as a family or aren't we? This isn't the time to be prideful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: With that, I don't disagree either. Nevertheless, our family seems incapable of standing united for long. If we could... this war never would have started.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall ensure this area remains secure for now; make your advance!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Continue your ascent, as I shall keep what passes for the Titan's armies occupied.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: They never seem to learn, do they? But neither do they ever seem to stop.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust that our defenses have not proven too much of a hindrance to you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: When our family stands united, there's no hardship that we cannot overcome.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I've wrath to spare for all those you shall confront on your way to the summit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Make haste toward the Palace, Cousin, and my blessing be with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Another victory is within reach; go take it, and I'll cover you as you proceed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Battle rages all around us still, Melinoë. Then let us finish what our enemies began.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: This mountain stronghold of ours shall never fall, try as the Titan might.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Climb forth, Cousin, and may your foothold never falter on the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Go quickly but with care; my father and the rest await you at the summit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm grateful that you're back, so that the next stage of our plan can now unfold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You're all the reinforcements we require, and I'm ever-grateful that you're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that we could fight together, yet we accomplish more going our separate ways.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Our foes swarm at us like insects, and we shall deal with them as such.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew that I could count on you to be here tonight precisely at this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Many such victories are still to come before our war is finally ended.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It is not enough merely to vanquish our enemies; let us make an example of them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The peak of Olympus awaits; go, and I'll ensure you need not watch your back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you've scores to settle in the Underworld, so thank you for being here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your skill alone has brought you to this point, although perhaps I may assist.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Let our enemies rue the moment they deigned to stand against we gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It is as though the Moon herself directed me to your position here tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Our family has endured for aeons; this shall pass, as does all else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: All those who dare oppose us or our rightful rule shall be given no quarter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Proceed immediately to the Palace and I'll cover you, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: What use is a good strategy without the necessary skill to see it through?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan's forces know nothing of fear, but I trust that they can still be taught.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: May the Boon I now bestow provide a difference in the battles yet to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Choose swiftly, Cousin, as there's another pack of uninvited guests I'd better intercept.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Well this is certainly a change of scenery from Mount Olympus, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: If ever I grow weary of Olympus, one look at this place shall make me long for it again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your victory last night predicts another soon to come, especially now that I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Our enemies ought to have learned they stand no chance against us anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The air hangs heavy, and our enemies are coursing with new strength. Let us beware.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Let no trace remain of the Father of All Monsters, for all the harm that he has wrought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: If this is how we shall assert our dominance for ages hence, then so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that I could be in several places all at once, but I chose a good one here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Our family is as close to perfect as can be, but let us strive to become greater still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to see a friendly face. Fending off the Titan's legions can be solitary work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Each such battle is nothing; yet we must pace ourselves for a protracted fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You surely could have handled all those wretches well enough on your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: No need to trade extensive pleasantries. Let's each of us remain on task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I had anticipated that you'd soon return. I trust the Underworld work proceeded well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I cannot reach your Underworld easily or for long, but know you'll keep it well under control.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not forget about my Amulet should you have need of me elsewhere, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: May all those who threaten our divine rule suffer tenfold for their arrogance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The noble Princess of the Dead, at last! I am your cousin Athena, and proud to welcome you to Olympus. Yet I could be prouder, as this wasn't always such a miserable place.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hail, Lady Athena, and well met! Please call me Melinoë. Now where can I direct my wrath, that we may liberate your mountain stronghold and see its glory all restored?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Well met indeed, Melinoë. The wretched Titan's armies have infiltrated our most sacred grounds. On them, your wrath may be well-spent; especially as you rise toward the summit, where most of our family now takes refuge.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Palace of Zeus. And what of you, why aren't the others out here fighting by your side?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Because that is precisely what our strategy requires, for they aided your arrival, did they not? Now, let us go our separate ways, and may my blessing aid in your ascent.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena, while we have a moment, I wish to offer you this Nectar as a token of my gratitude. If I'd only known that we would meet here, perhaps I'd have arrived even sooner.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Nonsense, for I'm certain that you made your journey here as quickly as you could. Well, I of course accept your offering, though shall be saving it for when our victory's achieved. This, however, you may be able to use sooner...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh. Try as I might to consider each possibility, partaking of Nectar in the midst of this conflict that we're in is quite unthinkable. Still, it's something to look forward to.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If it's something to look forward to, then it's a greater gift than I thought. The idea of a sip of Nectar while at peace... it does sound good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your gesture is unnecessary. Warring mortals often make me offerings in hopes that I might guide them to victory. But you and I have a shared purpose, so I require no such incentives in our case.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is no incentive, merely a way of saying thank you. For accepting me, and recognizing what potential I may have to aid our family in a time of need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You honor me, and have my thanks. This Nectar, it's been to the Underworld and back... along with you. It'll take on a particularly complex flavor, I should think.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If this bottle indeed traveled to the ends of the earth before returning home, then it's all the more suited to someone of your wisdom and experience. May you enjoy it once you lay down your arms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: At this rate, we might someday have ourselves a feast to rival one arranged by Dionysus himself! Then let us fight with all our might to bring about such an enticing possibility.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Here's to a future where you've time enough for feasting, not just military strategy. Though I'd not want to see you battle Dionysus, even when it comes to organizing feasts!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd think being surrounded by family would mean being surrounded by like-minded folk, except... not many on Olympus think as I do. None, I daresay. Yet you... well, one difference is, I'd never deign give Nectar at a time like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena, I am honored to have joined your fight, and to have earned your blessings and your faith. I sometimes overlook the need to express my gratitude, and so rely on this Nectar to do it for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Let it be said that we are goddesses of action, not words; and I accept your thoughtful, if oddly timed, offering. Though, let this be the last until our foes all lie in ruin.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have something of a symbolic gift for you, Lady Athena, for I know Ambrosia shall flow in abundance on Olympus again soon. May this particular bottle contribute to the restoration, and remind you of our victories.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I need no reminders of our victories, nor shall your generosity be forgotten during or after the restoration that inevitably is to come. What would we have done if not for you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Olympians have reigned for aeons, overcoming innumerable threats and setbacks. All this is but another; you would have figured out a way. Especially you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So adept at deflecting compliments even without my Boon. Then very well! Better to be modest than a braggart, I suppose. But you might aspire to be somewhere in between. Either that, or we should all be more like you.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Let's all be who we are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I've something here for you, Cousin. Seeing as you remain instrumental to our mountain's ongoing defense.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm honored, Lady Athena. A family such as ours ought to stand together during times of need. I wish to do my part.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You have impressed not only me but all our kin; most of them, I should say. And for that, I bestow my greatest power. May it soon bring about still greater victories.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You're injured, Cousin... though I shouldn't be so surprised. It is a grueling climb to reach this point, and you traversed the Rift of Thessaly as well. Are you all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can press on in this state, Lady Athena. Though whether I've the strength to surpass Prometheus, let alone what awaits beyond... that I can't guarantee.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: In war there are no guarantees save bloodshed, as Ares surely would attest, wherever he may be rejoicing. Now let's get you back to proper fighting shape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I had considered how we would proceed if by some chance the Three Fates were never found. What I did not anticipate was that they would be found, yet still take leave of their responsibilities... so I am told.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They've been weaving for aeons, and perhaps felt weary or even underappreciated. That, or they liked what they saw while their weavings were on hold. They did give us a big new prophecy as a sort of parting gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: A prophecy about another age, that we gods shall relinquish our dominion to mortals... it's difficult to believe. But then, these things never unfold precisely as they sound.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No... and for that matter, I couldn't tell whether the Fates were angry, or disappointed, or anything like that... on the contrary, they seemed enthusiastic.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: They are weavers, whose tapestries reveal the vast and complicated tale of history. Perhaps they seek merely to entertain, and we are but characters they have to work with.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So many battles have been fought and many yet remain. But we goddesses are tireless. You can count on me for as long as you choose to press on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Choose to press on... at times I feel obligated, Lady Athena. The possibilities of Typhon and Chronos... who shall stop them if not us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: A proper strategy requires taking every reasonable precaution, but can also be adapted should the need arise. Let us account for as many possibilities as we see fit, but never to the detriment of other matters of concern.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: We have achieved a monumental victory, Cousin. Although the annihilation of one's enemies can be a serviceable outcome in war, surrender often is preferred. I trust the others have expressed their gratitude?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, of course. And congratulations to you all, Lady Athena. My only regret is that this siege cannot immediately cease, given what's left to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Abandon that regret, for the rabble that still plagues Olympus is nothing. As for Prometheus, though I am skeptical about his role in this new strategy, I have persuaded Father to go along with it, for now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Now you are to confront these traces of our great foes, both at the summit and in Tartarus... re-creating the path that got you there, as though the possibilities need to align...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's how our grandfather explains it, more or less. Almost like we're circumventing outcomes in which we might otherwise have failed for one reason or another.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: There ought to be very few such outcomes then. For we do not fail; merely take the necessary time to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to know your family in the Underworld is safe, as well as Nyx and those who served the House of Hades. For all our troubles here, at least we were not divided... more than usual.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos took his wrath out on my father, but my mother and the rest were imprisoned in an instant... frozen in time. They awakened with no knowledge of this whole ordeal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps they are the most fortunate ones in all this. Though we shall have to be patient with their many questions.
